metadata.title: Dialog — the Thiel/Hoffman private invitation-only elite forum ('Bilderberg meets Silicon Valley'), its operating entity, membership machinery, AI member-dossiers, and the June-2026 leak (documented vs claimed, dated)
metadata.status: The society's existence (founded 2006), its co-founders (Peter Thiel, Auren Hoffman), executive director (Raffi Grinberg), the June-2026 leak (maia arson crimew -> WIRED, 16 Jun 2026: 113 names in dialog.org page source + a 222-person 2026 retreat list), the A/B/C member grading, the AI-assisted member dossiers (AUM, value-add score, political leanings) stored in Airtable, the graded >$10k fees, and the reported Northern-Virginia campus intent are DOCUMENTED (WIRED, Forbes, Byline Times, Axios, Semafor, NATO Defense College Foundation, Irish Times). The operating entity Stonebrick LLC (owned by Hoffman) rests on a single investigative outlet + public-records inference. The 'Epstein invited 2014' claim is RETRACTED (different Jeff Epstein). Attendance/membership != wrongdoing.
metadata.frame: Dialog is a private, invitation-only society co-founded in 2006 by Peter Thiel and data-broker Auren Hoffman, often described as 'Bilderberg meets Silicon Valley.' It runs off-the-record retreats (2,500+ lifetime attendees) plus a paid membership (1,000+, target ~1,300 by end-2026). Correcting the popular framing: Dialog is OPERATED by Stonebrick, LLC — a FOR-PROFIT company owned by Hoffman (Mamaroneck, NY), not a chartered society/nonprofit; Thiel is the namesake co-founder but reportedly 'has no hand in running' it; executive director Raffi Grinberg runs day-to-day ops (with staffer/managing director Juliette Levine). Members are graded A/B/C by staff AND AI tools on influence, wealth (assets under management), fame, and 'political fit'; the grade drives retreat pricing (full price can exceed $10,000; a leaked 2022 California invite showed $16,846, ~70% discounted by code), dinner seating, and reinvitation. Every member/prospect gets an AI-assisted DOSSIER (AUM, a 1-4 value-add score, moderation-trust, culture-fit, staff-adjusted political leanings, plus heavy PII and a dating/matchmaking arm) stored in a commercial Airtable — which is exactly what leaked. Retreats are held at rented luxury/secluded venues (Ritz-Carlton Bacara, Dove Mountain; San Clemente Palace, Venice; the 2026 Powerscourt/Ireland retreat was cancelled after the leak). Dialog is reported (Axios, Aug 2025) to be moving to a permanent campus in the Northern-Virginia suburbs near Washington. This block attaches Dialog as a PURE-SINK overlay hub to already-modeled nodes (Palantir, Founders Fund, a16z, OpenAI, Anduril, PsiQuantum, BlackRock, Elon Musk) plus its principals and operating entity.
metadata.leak: On 15 Jun 2026 Swiss hacktivist maia arson crimew (prior: US No Fly List exposure, Verkada breach) found 113 member names hardcoded in plaintext in dialog.org's page source; the data sat in an inadequately access-controlled Airtable reachable by completing a web form (no intrusion needed). She tipped WIRED, which verified and published 16 Jun 2026; a separate source provided WIRED the 222-person registration list for the 2026 retreat (Powerscourt, near Dublin; 87 first-timers). Exposed fields: bios, home cities/addresses, emails/mobiles, birthdates, assistants' contacts, political leanings, matchmaking data (dating.dialog.org), and private access tokens usable as credentials (WIRED withheld). Dialog called it an 'attack' by a 'well-known criminal wanted in the United States'; WIRED found no break-in was necessary. Security analysts flagged the corpus as a ready-made target list for espionage/influence/blackmail. grade: documented.
metadata.grade_direction_dispute: The A/B/C grade DIRECTION is contested across outlets: WIRED-sourced reporting (Washington Times, squaredtech, AI Weekly) frames C as the TOP tier (most famous/influential, ~1 in 7), B as the default majority (141/192 in the leak), and A as a 'polite holding pen' for older, established-but-less-notable members; other outlets imply the reverse. What is well documented and NOT in dispute: a wealth/fame/influence-weighted ranking EXISTS and sets price, seating, and reinvitation. Presented as conflicting accounts, not resolved.
metadata.composition_guard: Do NOT read Dialog as a single unitary 'mind' or impute the group's ethos/politics to any individual attendee (composition/division error). The documented findings are about institutional STRUCTURE and CONDUCT: a for-profit operating entity; an AI-driven member-grading/dossier apparatus; a leaked directory; an off-the-record norm without NDAs. A senator, a DNI, or an actor appearing in the directory is a data point about the CONVENING, not evidence of shared belief or wrongdoing. WIRED itself cautioned the 113-name directory does not distinguish member vs participant vs past guest.
metadata.the_honest_reading: Dialog is a two-decade-old, Thiel-branded but Hoffman-run (Stonebrick LLC) invitation-only forum that convenes a cross-elite roster — tech/VC founders, defense-AI principals, senators, agency heads, academics, and culture figures — under an engineered off-the-record norm, while privately grading and dossiering those same people by wealth, fame, and 'political fit' via AI tools. The defining tension is doctrinal: an in-room 'avoid status signalling' ethos layered over a rigorously quantified status apparatus — best read as function (status settled administratively so it need not be performed), not hypocrisy. The June-2026 leak — 113 names in plaintext page source, a 222-person retreat list, dossiers with AUM/politics/matchmaking data in an open Airtable — turned a secrecy-as-identity society into a documented case study, and is what makes this mappable at all. Overlay; excluded from the formal financial proofs. Presumption of innocence throughout; the Epstein/2014 claim is retracted and not repeated.

Forbes — What we know about billionaire Peter Thiel's secret Dialog society (Jun 18, 2026)
Byline Times — Peter Thiel's least secret network is one small part of a global influence machine (Jun 23, 2026)
The Dissent — Thiel's secret society wasn't very secret: Dialog member list exposed in plaintext
Security Affairs — Peter Thiel secret-society leak creates a perfect target list for espionage/blackmail
squaredtech — Peter Thiel's Dialog club secretly grades members by wealth and fame
Axios — Dialog, the secret network from Thiel and Hoffman, eyes a permanent D.C.-area campus (Aug 7, 2025)
Semafor — Private club founded by Peter Thiel, Auren Hoffman eyes campus near DC (Aug 8, 2025)
NATO Defense College Foundation (Spagnuolo) — Dialog on AI systems: shifting warfare and powers
Irish Times — Wicklow hotel cancels secretive Peter Thiel conference (Jul 3, 2026)
